evidence.scientific_clarity: Confusion matrix renders correctly with diagonal annotated in bold white on green and off-diagonal shaded in Blues; dual-colormap scheme deviates from brief's single-sequential-cmap instruction, slightly reducing interpretive clarity.
evidence.typography: Script does not set pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype=0; PDF contains Type3 (bitmap) fonts, text is not selectable; SVG uses DejaVu Sans (matplotlib default), not a publication-quality font.
evidence.palette: Blues (off-diagonal) and Greens (diagonal) are both colorblind-safe sequential colormaps; however, using green to mark 'correct' cells introduces directional/semantic color coding not requested by the brief.
evidence.layout_density: 7x6 figure fills space reasonably; colorbar is present and labeled; tick labels are visible without overlap; no excessive whitespace.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption correctly describes rows=true classes, columns=predicted classes, diagonal annotation, and Blues colormap for errors; includes a specific numerical claim (accuracy range 69%-92%); caption matches the figure.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF confirmed to use Type3 fonts (bitmap-encoded glyphs); script never sets matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype']=42, so text is not selectable or editable in the PDF.
evidence.file_format: All required outputs present: figure.pdf (25 KB), figure.png (non-trivial), figure.svg (60 KB), caption.tex, gen_figure.py — all non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names axis semantics, explains diagonal vs off-diagonal color coding, and provides a quantitative accuracy range (69%-92%); does not mention the vmax scale anchor or the Greens colormap used for the diagonal.

overall_strengths: All five required output files are present and non-trivial, satisfying the file-format checklist completely.
Caption includes a specific numerical finding (per-class accuracy range 69%-92%) and clearly explains the dual-colormap design, matching the rendered figure.
overall_weaknesses: pdf.fonttype=42 is never set, resulting in Type3 (bitmap) fonts in the PDF and DejaVu Sans defaults in the SVG — text is not selectable or editable.

The brief asked for a single perceptually-uniform colormap (viridis/inferno/Blues) applied to the whole matrix; instead, two colormaps are used (Blues + Greens), and the Greens diagonal introduces unrequested directional/semantic color coding.
